Perfect. Here's **Phase 2** — the markdown page system upgrade that builds directly on top of your Phase 1 Mission Log.

This will give every mission its own auto-generated, linkable, and editable page — just like Obsidian.

---

## **PHASE 2: MARKDOWN PAGE SYSTEM (Obsidian-Style Expansion)**

```plaintext
Enhance the Mission Log system by implementing a dynamic markdown page engine for every mission. Each task becomes its own page, stored, linkable, and editable like in Obsidian.

---

### 1. AUTO-GENERATED PAGES FOR EACH MISSION

- When a mission is created (via input or added to the log):
  - Automatically generate a **dedicated page** stored by its title.
  - Example: “Morning Routine” → creates `/pages/morning-routine`
  - Page content should include:
    - Mission title
    - Body/content field (rich text or markdown-style)
    - Metadata (e.g. XP, created date, tags, completed: true/false)

---

### 2. PAGE STRUCTURE & EDITING

Each mission page should:
- Be **editable** like a notes document
- Support **basic markdown** or rich text:
  - `# Heading`
  - `- Bullet list`
  - `[[Internal Links]]` (Phase 3 for backlinks)
- Include a “Save” button that persists the content in local storage or a database.

---

### 3. PAGE VIEW ROUTING (STATIC OR SPA)

Enable routing so clicking a mission title or **“View Full Report”** from the info popup opens its page.

- If using a single-page app (SPA): Use dynamic views or modals.
- If multi-page: Use `/pages/:title` structure.
- Display the full editable content and metadata.

---

### 4. PAGE STORAGE SYSTEM

- Each page must be stored in an object or lightweight database as:
```js
{
  title: "Morning Routine",
  slug: "morning-routine",
  content: "## Objectives\n- Wake early\n- Stretch\n...",
  createdAt: "2025-04-16T09:00:00",
  completed: false,
  xp: 10,
  tags: ["Routine", "Wellness"]
}
```

- Store using:
  - **LocalStorage** (MVP)
  - Or expandable to Firebase, Supabase, or file-based system later

---

### 5. INLINE LINK SUPPORT (OPTIONAL START)

Add inline support for:
- Typing `[[Another Mission]]` creates/links to that page
- Autocomplete suggestion (optional)
- Clicking the link takes the user to that page

---

### 6. UI DESIGN SUGGESTIONS

- Editor: textarea or markdown editor with real-time preview
- Metadata visible in a side column or header
- Page has “Back to Mission Log” button
- Styled like Obsidian: clean, dark/light toggle, minimal UI chrome

---

### 7. HOOK INTO EXISTING MISSION LOG

- When a user creates a new mission:
  - Auto-create and link its page in the background
- When a user clicks a mission or info modal’s **[View Full Report]**:
  - Open its linked page
- Marking it complete should update both the Mission Log and the page metadata (`completed: true`)

---

### FUTURE HOOKS (DO NOT BUILD YET):
- Backlinking graph between pages
- Embedded content (`![[Page]]`)
- Rich block embeds (quotes, images, task checklists)
```

---

When you're ready, I can generate the **code scaffold** for:
- Mission Log + auto page creation
- Basic markdown editor
- LocalStorage page manager

Would you like it in **React**, **Vanilla JS**, or a full **Next.js stack**?
